page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-heavy server instance. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The architecture is x86_64 with dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across four drives, but does not contain any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-threaded decompression performance, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Image processing via Geekbench also ranks in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 support enable strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference, specifically in prompt processing and text generation for small 135M models. Other benchmarks, including single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average middle 50% range.

The high density of local NVMe SSD storage combined with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance class highly resource-dense. The qualitative cost efficiency is characterized by the integration of massive local storage and substantial memory, reducing the need for external network-attached storage. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for large-scale deep learning training, though it remains capable of smaller CPU-based inference. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd is an x86_64 server configured with 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. This instance features 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across four drives, with no integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, image processing, and CPU-based LLM inference for small models, utilizing built-in Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions. Other metrics, such as memory bandwidth and Redis operations, perform in the average tier. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density by bundling massive local storage with a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio, minimizing external storage dependencies. This configuration is suited for database hosting, data-intensive processing, and workloads requiring rapid local storage access.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD. It delivers strong multi-threaded decompression and CPU-based small LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-32-highlssd is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 12,884 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring massive local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM on dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors
12,884 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-threaded decompression and image processing performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated CPU-based LLM inference
Suited for high-throughput databases and memory-intensive data processing
